methodsAttempted.query: site:kent.ac.uk University of Kent generative AI policy artificial intelligence students assessment academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl official-domain search found the public student AI guidance cluster, the AI Principles page, the AI and Academic Integrity page, and the formal Academic Integrity and Misconduct DOCX.

methodsAttempted.query: site:kent.ac.uk University of Kent AI guidance staff students ChatGPT generative AI assessment
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl official-domain search found the ChatGPT Edu announcement, public student guidance pages, and a public research guidance page.

methodsAttempted.query: site:kent.ac.uk University of Kent information security generative AI confidential data ChatGPT
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl official-domain search found the public Data Privacy guidance page; research repository results were rejected because they were research outputs rather than university policy or guidance.
Public official student pages were fetched with Firecrawl and used only where the page text supported a narrow claim.
Staff data-protection and AI@Kent FAQ links resolved to Kent SharePoint locations, but they require Kent authentication and were recorded as inaccessible rather than used as evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source-only discovery found a public University of Kent AI guidance cluster and related official policy/guidance sources. No single central binding university-wide AI policy page was found; the public AI Principles page states that an AI Policy Group is undertaking a university-wide policy review.

targets.sourceTitle: Free ChatGPT Edu access coming soon for all students!

stopConditions: use only public official Kent pages and documents
record SharePoint and Moodle sources as inaccessible rather than bypassing login
do not stage raw html docx pdf or screenshots
do not infer a central binding AI policy where public sources provide principles and guidance

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The page is an official gateway to AI guidance pages, but it mostly summarizes and links to narrower pages used in this run.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Free ChatGPT Edu access coming soon for all students!

artifacts.claimText: The University of Kent publishes AI principles that set expectations for responsible, ethical, lawful and effective engagement with AI across education, research and professional services.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: These principles set out the University’s expectations for responsible, ethical, lawful and effective engagement with AI across education, research and professional services.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow public principles/guidance claim, not as a binding formal AI policy.

artifacts.claimText: Kent's AI principles say academic judgement about student work remains with academic staff, and AI will not be used to make marking or academic-outcome decisions unless explicitly authorised, clearly communicated, pedagogically justified, and subject to human oversight.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: AI will not be used to make decisions about marks or academic outcomes unless its use is explicitly authorised, clearly communicated, pedagogically justified, and always with human oversight.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim closely tracks mandatory wording in the principles page.

artifacts.claimText: Kent's student AI academic-integrity guidance says that, unless specifically instructed otherwise, submitted assessment content must always be the student's own work and students should not include AI-generated material in submissions.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Unless it is otherwise noted, you should: 1. Not include materials generated by AI in your submissions. 2. Not submit materials that you have written but that have been substantially altered by AI.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: What Your Own Work Means When Using AI

artifacts.notes: Approved as student-facing academic integrity guidance; wording is scoped to submitted assessments and the default unless otherwise noted.

artifacts.claimText: Kent's Academic Integrity and Misconduct policy defines unauthorised AI use as using generative AI beyond the scope permitted for a specific assessment, or failing to acknowledge permitted use appropriately.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Unauthorised use of artificial intelligence: Use of generative AI beyond the scope permitted for the specific assessment, or failure to appropriately acknowledge its use where allowed.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the formal academic integrity policy explicitly defines unauthorised AI use.

artifacts.claimText: Kent's public AI data-privacy guidance tells users not to enter personal information, confidential information, sensitive data, or other people's work into AI tools, and specifically says personal or sensitive data should never be put into ChatGPT Edu.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Furthermore, you should never put personal or sensitive data into ChatGPT Edu. For these reasons, you should not enter personal information, confidential information or other people’s work into an AI tool.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the public guidance contains direct data-handling instructions for AI tools and ChatGPT Edu.

artifacts.claimText: Kent's public research guidance says researchers using GenAI to process data must follow the University Data Protection Policy, complete DPIA screening where relevant, record planned GenAI use in data management plans, and document GenAI use.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: When using a GenAI tool to process data you must abide by the existing University of Kent Data Protection Policy, and carry out a Data Protection Impact Assessment Screening Questionnaire.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: What issues do I need to consider if I use GenAI in my research?

artifacts.notes: Approved as public research guidance, not as a broader staff-only SharePoint policy claim.

artifacts.claimText: Kent's public Microsoft Copilot guidance says Copilot Chat is available to all students and staff using a Kent IT account, while warning users to double-check outputs and consult module convenors before using generative AI tools in assignments.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: This tool is available to all students and staff at the University of Kent. Copilot Chat uses publicly accessible material from the internet to generate its responses.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a tool-availability and use-caution claim based on a public official Kent page.

artifacts.claimText: A Kent Student News announcement says the University is collaborating with OpenAI to give all students and staff free access to ChatGPT Edu, with access for students planned for April 2026.

citation.citationTitle: Free ChatGPT Edu access coming soon for all students!

artifacts.sourceTitle: Free ChatGPT Edu access coming soon for all students!
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The University of Kent is collaborating with OpenAI to give all students and staff free access to ChatGPT Edu – a version of ChatGPT built for universities.

citation.citationTitle: Free ChatGPT Edu access coming soon for all students!

artifacts.notes: Approved as an official announcement/tool-access claim; not treated as a binding policy source.

artifacts.claimText: The public AI Principles page says Kent's AI Policy Group is undertaking a university-wide review of current policies, so this run did not identify a completed central binding AI policy page beyond published principles and related guidance.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The AI Policy Group is undertaking a university-wide review of current policies in light of developments in AI tools and their potential application.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a source-status limitation, grounded in the page's policy-review statement and the official-source discovery results.

artifacts.title: University of Kent staged AI policy crawl
artifacts.summary: Official public Kent sources show AI principles for responsible, ethical, lawful and effective AI use, student academic-integrity guidance, a formal academic integrity policy definition of unauthorised AI use, public privacy guidance, public research guidance, and tool-access guidance for Copilot and ChatGPT Edu. The run did not identify a completed central binding AI policy page; the public AI Principles page says a university-wide policy review is underway.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
SharePoint and Moodle guidance linked from public Kent pages was not accessed because it requires authentication.
No central completed binding AI policy page was found in public official sources during this run; public claims are scoped to the specific source pages and documents.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
